eco_baseline_footprint.formula: BIF = (vCPU x 15 EUR) + (Storage GB x 0.08 EUR)
eco_baseline_footprint.method_note: The MCC assumes a 3-node MongoDB replica set with 2 vCPUs and 60 GB of storage per node, corresponding to 6 total vCPUs and 180 GB of total baseline storage. RAM is considered included in compute cost and is not monetized separately in the framework cost model.
eco_baseline_footprint.source: Framework cost model applied to MongoDB Minimum Correctness Configuration (3-node replica set: 1 primary and 2 secondary nodes)

ps_throughput.estimation_note: Throughput estimate for the adopted 3-node replica-set baseline under default write concern w:1, used for framework-level classification rather than as a maximum benchmark claim. The value reflects a throughput-oriented operational estimate, not a majority-confirmed durability setting.
ps_throughput.source: MongoDB replication architecture and replica-set operational model

eco_variable_transaction_cost.source: MongoDB self-managed database model: no native protocol transaction fee

eco_storage_footprint.method_note: Baseline storage footprint for the adopted MCC, assuming 60 GB of replicated storage per node across a 3-node replica set.
eco_storage_footprint.source: Framework MCC assumption: 3-node replica set with 60 GB of storage per node

parallel_execution.motivation: In the adopted replica-set baseline, MongoDB does not provide native parallel execution across independent execution domains; writes are accepted by a single primary node.

horizontal_partitioning.motivation: The adopted Minimum Correctness Configuration excludes sharding, so no native horizontal workload partitioning is assumed in the benchmark baseline.

absence_of_global_serialization.motivation: In the adopted replica-set baseline, write ordering remains centralized through one elected primary rather than partitioned across multiple independent execution domains.

scalable_coordination_model.motivation: Under the MCC, write coordination is centered on a single primary and does not provide a positive structural scalability contribution via distributed execution partitioning or validator-level parallelism.

ps_scalability.source: MongoDB replica-set MCC baseline (3-node replica set without sharding) as defined in the benchmark framework

append_only_structural_history.motivation: No protocol-native immutable append-only ledger is enforced; data can be updated or deleted and the oplog functions as a replication log rather than as a structurally tamper-evident ledger history.

cryptographic_linkage_between_records.motivation: MongoDB does not natively provide ledger-style cryptographic chaining between records.

cryptographic_write_authentication.motivation: MongoDB provides authentication and authorization, but not transaction-level cryptographic attestation of each record write as a structural ledger property.

independent_integrity_verification.motivation: There is no protocol-native independent integrity-verification mechanism comparable to distributed-ledger validation.

rci_tamper_evidence_auditability.source: MongoDB auditing, authentication, and replication model

protocol_enforced_irreversible_commit.motivation: Irreversible commit is not structurally enforced in the adopted baseline configuration. Under the default w:1 write concern, the primary acknowledges writes before replication to secondaries is confirmed, so rollback remains possible under documented failure and election conditions.

multi_party_confirmation_requirement.motivation: The adopted replica-set architecture supports multi-node confirmation when stronger write concerns such as majority are used, even though this is not part of the throughput-oriented DOM baseline.

absence_of_unilateral_rollback_capabilit.motivation: Rollback scenarios remain possible under documented failover and replication conditions.

rci_finality_rollback_resistance.source: MongoDB rollback and write concern model

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.configuration_note: Score refers strictly to the MCC with three data-bearing members in a standard replica set.

absence_of_single_writer_requirement.motivation: Only the elected primary receives write operations in a replica set.

quorum_based_write_continuation.motivation: Replica-set elections and write availability depend on majority/quorum semantics.

automatic_write_continuation_mechanism.motivation: Replica sets provide automatic failover through election of a new primary after eligible failure scenarios.

multi_node_write_survivability.motivation: A standard three-member replica set provides redundancy and write continuity if quorum is preserved.

rci_write_availability_fault_tolerance.source: MongoDB replication and replica-set architecture documentation

absence_of_single_entity_unilateral_stat.motivation: Replica sets are normally controlled administratively by a single organization or operator.

structural_requirement_for_multi_entity_.motivation: Replication across nodes does not constitute independent multi-entity validation.

validator_admission_independence.motivation: Node membership is configured and controlled by the operator; there is no permissionless or independently governed validator admission.

absence_of_structurally_fixed_validator_.motivation: The set of replica-set members is explicitly administered and fixed by configuration choices.

native_privilege_differentiation_capabil.motivation: MongoDB supports authentication and role-based authorization.

native_identity_lifecycle_governance_cap.motivation: MongoDB supports managed user identities and access-control configuration for deployments.

structural_independence_of_identity_gove.motivation: Identity governance remains under operator control and is not structurally decentralized across independent entities.

ags_collusion_resilience.configuration_note: The score reflects the fact that the replica set remains under a single administrative domain. Majority election semantics do not imply collusion resilience across independent governance actors.

non_unilateral_compromise_requirement.motivation: A sufficiently privileged administrator or operator can unilaterally compromise the deployment, so non-unilateral compromise is not structurally required.

majority_compromise_required.motivation: Replica-set majority semantics support elections and write availability, but they do not impose a majority compromise threshold against an administrator operating within the same administrative domain.

supermajority_compromise_required.motivation: No supermajority compromise threshold is structurally enforced by the architecture for administrative control over the system.

ags_collusion_resilience.source: MongoDB replica-set write concern and election model
